verses.t: Whereas many great teachings have been given to us through the law and the neḇi'im and the others that followed them, on account of which we should praise Yasharal for instruction and wisdom; and since it is necessary not only that the readers themselves should acquire understanding but also that those who love learning should be able to help the outsiders by both speaking and writing, my grandfather Yahusha, after devoting himself especially to the reading of the law and the neḇi'im and the other books of our fathers, and after acquiring considerable proficiency in them, was himself also led to write something pertaining to instruction and wisdom, in order that, by becoming conversant with this also, those who love learning should make even greater progress in living according to the law.
The sand of the sea, the drops of rain, and the days of eternity -- who can count them?

Whereas many great teachings have been given to us through the law and the neḇi'im and the others that followed them, on account of which we should praise Yasharal for instruction and wisdom; and since it is necessary not only that the readers themselves should acquire understanding but also that those who love learning should be able to help the outsiders by both speaking and writing, my grandfather Yahusha, after devoting himself especially to the reading of the law and the neḇi'im and the other books of our fathers, and after acquiring considerable proficiency in them, was himself also led to write something pertaining to instruction and wisdom, in order that, by becoming conversant with this also, those who love learning should make even greater progress in living according to the law.
The sand of the sea, the drops of rain, and the days of eternity -- who can count them?

The sand of the sea, the drops of rain, and the days of eternity -- who can count them?

The sand of the sea, the drops of rain, and the days of eternity -- who can count them?
The height of shamayim, the breadth of the earth, the abyss, and wisdom -­ who can search them out?
Wisdom was created before all things, and prudent understanding from eternity.
The root of wisdom -- to whom has it been revealed? Her clever devices­ - who knows them?
There is One who is wise, greatly to be feared, sitting upon his throne.
Yahuah himself created wisdom; he saw her and apportioned her, he poured her out upon all his works.
She dwells with all flesh according to his gift, and he supplied her to those who love him.
The fear of Yahuah is glory and exultation, and gladness and a crown of rejoicing.
The fear of Yahuah delights the heart, and gives gladness and joy and long life.
With him who fears Yahuah it will go well at the end; on the day of his death he will be baruk.
To fear Yahuah is the beginning of wisdom; she is created with the faithful in the womb.
She made among men an eternal foundation, and among their descendants she will be trusted.
To fear Yahuah is wisdom's full measure; she satisfies men with her fruits;
she fills their whole house with desirable goods, and their storehouses with her produce.
The fear of Yahuah is the crown of wisdom, making peace and perfect health to flourish.
He saw her and apportioned her; he rained down knowledge and discerning comprehension, and he exalted the glory of those who held her fast.
To fear Yahuah is the root of wisdom, and her branches are long life.
Unrighteous anger cannot be justified, for a man's anger tips the scale to his ruin.
A patient man will endure until the right moment, and then joy will burst forth for him.
He will hide his words until the right moment, and the lips of many will tell of his good sense.
In the treasuries of wisdom are wise sayings, but godliness is an abomination to a sinner.
If you desire wisdom, keep the mitsvot, and Yahuah will supply it for you.
For the fear of Yahuah is wisdom and instruction, and he delights in fidelity and meekness.
Do not disobey the fear of Yahuah; do not approach him with a divided mind.
Be not a hypocrite in men's sight, and keep watch over your lips.

Do not exalt yourself lest you fall, and thus bring dishonor upon yourself. Yahuah will reveal your secrets and cast you down in the midst of the qahal, because you did not come in the fear of Yahuah, and your heart was full of deceit.
Whoever requites favors gives thought to the future; at the moment of his falling he will find support.
If you are willing, my son, you will be taught, and if you apply yourself you will become clever.
If you love to listen you will gain knowledge, and if you incline your ear you will become wise.
Stand in the qahal of the elders. Who is wise? Hold fast to him.
Be ready to listen to every narrative, and do not let wise proverbs escape you.
If you see an intelligent man, visit him early; let your foot wear out his doorstep.
Reflect on the statutes of Yahuah, and meditate at all times on his mitsvot. It is he who will give insight to your mind, and your desire for wisdom will be granted.
